name: Mo Money Pawn Shop
description_short: Family-owned Phoenix pawn shop with 35 years in business offering firearms, jewelry, precious metals, and electronics. Licensed firearms dealer with online shopping and interest-free layaway.
description_long:
Mo Money Pawn Shop is a family-owned pawn shop based in Phoenix, Arizona, that has operated for 35 years. The business has established itself as Arizona's largest pawn shop and positions itself as a customer-service-focused alternative to typical pawn shop experiences. The company operates both an in-store location (10am-7pm) and a night window (7pm-12am daily), alongside online sales channels including eBay and Buya.com marketplace listings.

The shop's primary services include purchasing and selling firearms as a licensed dealer, buying and selling jewelry (including broken, damaged, and estate pieces), precious metals trading (gold, silver, and platinum), and electronics appraisal and sales. They also operate Mo's Ice Cream inside the shop, offering Thrifty brand ice cream, homemade ice cream sandwiches, slushies, nachos, baked goods, and candy. The company provides a free layaway program with no interest charges and no fees—requiring only 25% down and 25% minimum payments every 30 days, though a 20% restocking fee applies to cancelled layaways.

The shop distinguishes itself through customer service emphasis and transparent pricing. Customer reviews highlight friendly, knowledgeable staff; the owner's willingness to resolve issues beyond standard business practice; special treatment for Air Force veterans (no charge for gun lower installations); and an overall approach that contrasts with negative stereotypes about pawn shops. The business maintains multiple online sales channels and emphasizes fair pricing for precious metals and electronics through expert appraisers.

However, this remains a traditional pawn shop model dependent on collateral-based transactions. While they offer layaway and competitive pricing, the core business revolves around short-term loans against personal goods and resale of secondhand merchandise. The company is not a lender in the traditional sense and does not offer credit products, making it unsuitable for customers seeking loans without collateral or credit-building services.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: 35 years in business with established reputation as Arizona's largest pawn shop
Interest-free layaway program with no fees (only 25% down, 25% minimum payments every 30 days)
Licensed firearms dealer with knowledgeable staff and special veteran discounts
Multiple sales channels including in-store, online, eBay, and Buya.com marketplace
Expert appraisers for fair pricing on precious metals, jewelry, electronics, and firearms
Extended hours with night window operation (7pm-12am daily)
Consistently positive customer reviews praising customer service and owner integrity
cons: 20% restocking fee on cancelled layaways reduces flexibility
Late payment consequences result in loss of layaway and invested funds with no grace period noted
Requires collateral to conduct business—not suitable for those seeking unsecured loans
Limited to Phoenix area for in-person transactions despite online options
Pawn shop model inherently carries stigma and may not serve customers seeking traditional financing
best_for: Phoenix-area residents seeking to pawn or sell firearms, jewelry, gold, or electronics with fair pricing
Customers needing interest-free layaway for purchases without breaking the bank
Military veterans seeking firearms services with special discounts
People with collateral (jewelry, firearms, electronics) who need immediate cash or prefer selling over pawning
diagnosis: Mo Money Pawn Shop is best for Phoenix-area residents who have collateral (jewelry, firearms, or electronics) and need immediate cash, want to sell items at fair market value, or prefer interest-free layaway purchases. The main caveat is that this is a traditional pawn shop requiring collateral—it cannot serve customers seeking unsecured loans or credit products, and all transactions are dependent on having valuable items to pledge or sell.
services: Jewelry buying (chains, rings, watches, damaged, estate pieces, dental gold)
Gold, silver, and platinum buying and selling
Interest-free layaway program (25% down, 25% minimum payments every 30 days)
Online inventory browsing and purchasing (website, eBay, Buya.com)
Mo's Ice Cream shop (Thrifty brand ice cream, homemade ice cream sandwiches, slushies, food items)
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 1152 E Indian School Rd, Phoenix, AZ 85014
